action.content: What are the best things to do in Rochester, New York? Can you write them as a song and use music notation in your response? Make sure to include the keywords 'festival' and 'river'.
action.label: context

action.content:
Here is a song about the best things to do in Rochester, New York, complete with lyrics and musical notation.

### **Title: The City of Flowers**
**Tempo:** Allegro (Lively)
**Time Signature:** 4/4

```text
| C   | G   | Am  | F   |
(Verse 1)
Wake up early, see the sun rise bright
Over the Genesee, a morning light
Walk the banks where the water flows
Watch the boats and the ducks go slow

| C   | G   | Am  | F   |
(Chorus)
Oh, Rochester, you are so grand
The best place in all the land
From the historic bridge to the park so green
The finest city that you've ever seen

| F   | G   | C   | G   |
(Bridge)
Don't miss the annual flower show display
A summer festival that brings the day
With music loud and the crowds so wide
By the river where the willows hide

| C   | G   | Am  | F   |
(Verse 2)
Visit the museum, learn of the past
See the history written in the glass
Then grab a bite at the foodie scene
The best pie in the whole machine

| C   | G   | Am  | F   |
(Chorus)
Oh, Rochester, you are so grand
The best place in all the land
From the historic bridge to the park so green
The finest city that you've ever seen

| F   | G   | C   | G   |
(Outro)
By the river, under the sky
Watch the clouds go floating by
Rochester, we love you so
The best city, you will always know

| C   | G   | Am  | F   | G   | C   |
(Final Chord)
```

**Musical Notes on the Notation:**
*   **Chords:** The numbers (C, G, Am, F) represent the chord progression. You can play these on a guitar, piano, or simply hum along to the rhythm.
*   **Rhythm:** The lyrics are written in a standard 4/4 time signature, meaning there are four beats per measure. The syllables generally align with the quarter notes.
*   **Keywords Included:** The word **"festival"** appears in the Bridge, and the word **"river"** appears in both the Bridge and the Outro.
action.label: response
